evidence.text: Good company and work life balance. Decent salary Good work life balance

Great work-life balance. This can be team dependent, but it is very rare to be pulled into work during off-hours or on the weekend. People are generally friendly, and are understanding of the need to prioritize family life over work when necessary. Great work life balance
Career Growth & Mobility - Tuition Reimbursement for continued education and certifications Great Benefits and Career Growth
Base pay and opportunity for growth Great environment and growth
Professionalism, support, training, benefits package... Great company, recent volatility in leadership
great people-learned a lot-very supportive management working for fidelity

Fidelity does provide room for growth, the benefits are good, the culture is very positive, the training is paid, they listen to their employees, and provide resources for career development Fair to Middling
Pretty good work environment and quality of life generally. Friendly coworkers that often really are open to getting to know you. Solid work environment and Supportive people

Fidelity offers a strong culture of integrity, collaboration, and continuous learning. Leadership is supportive and encourages data-driven decision-making. The company provides exposure to large-scale financial datasets and meaningful analytical projects that directly impact business outcomes. There are good growth opportunities, competitive benefits, and a stable work environment. Teams are generally collaborative,...

Lots of re-orgs on a regular basis, lots of changes in reporting structure moving to a model approach but creates a major disconnect and lack of leadership for employees. â¢ Toxic political culture within the Asset Management division. â¢ Even high-performing employees are subject to unfair treatment and poor reviews due to internal power dynamics. â¢ Concerns escalated to HR and senior leadership were completely dismissed â both acted more like enablers than problem solvers. â¢ HR is ineffective and not employee-focused; they protect toxic leadership rather than support fairne...
Favoritism, toxic work environment. Limited career opportunities unless you're one of their favorites Favoritism

Some of the management is toxic - very much a good ol' boys club Favoritism is rampant, and skill/expertise can be ignored in favor of it The new hires make almost as much as the people who have been there for years because they didn't adjust salaries for those who took cuts when converting to full time in previous years Inbound Operations is Toxic

Gaslighting clients to buy a managed solution rather than giving advice. Constantly understaffed with nonstop queue. Constantly changing KPIs. Gradually adding responsibilities with no pay increase. Promotability is mostly playing favorites. Decent company terrible department
Very hesitant to make change / experiment even when opportunities are fairly clear Politics can get in the way of making progress
